pattern: With every node having degree at most two and the graph connected, the whole thing is a single path or single cycle, and the largest labels should cluster in the middle (or around the ring) so big values multiply big values.
hints: A connected graph where each node touches at most two others is just one long chain, or one loop.
To maximize a sum of products of adjacent labels, the biggest numbers should sit next to each other; place the largest in the center and add the next ones alternately to each side.
Detect path versus cycle by comparing edge count to node count; lay out values n, n-1, ... outward from the middle, and for a cycle close the ring so the two smallest end up adjacent.

edge_inputs.description: path labelled so endpoints are the largest indices

stress.description: random single path with shuffled node labels
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 8))
    perm = list(range(n))
    rng.shuffle(perm)
    edges = [[perm[i], perm[i + 1]] for i in range(n - 1)]
    return [n, edges]
stress.note: kept small so the permutation-brute oracle stays fast

stress.description: random single cycle with shuffled node labels
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, min(size, 8))
    perm = list(range(n))
    rng.shuffle(perm)
    edges = [[perm[i], perm[(i + 1) % n]] for i in range(n)]
    return [n, edges]
